# clickhouse_api_key (Resource)

You can use the *clickhouse_api_key* resource to create and manage ClickHouse Cloud organization API keys.

The key secret is generated by ClickHouse Cloud and returned **only once, at creation time**. It is stored in Terraform state as a sensitive value (`key_secret`). Because the API never returns the secret again, an imported `clickhouse_api_key` will have an empty `key_secret`.

To assign RBAC roles (system or custom) to the key, use the `clickhouse_role_assignment` resource with `api_key_ids`. This resource does not manage roles directly.

## Example Usage

```hcl
resource "clickhouse_api_key" "monitoring" {
  name  = "prometheus-scraper"
  state = "enabled"

  ip_access = [
    {
      source      = "10.0.0.0/8"
      description = "vpc"
    },
  ]
}
```

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "clickhouse_api_key" "monitoring" {
  name  = "prometheus-scraper"
  state = "enabled"

  ip_access = [
    {
      source      = "10.0.0.0/8"
      description = "vpc"
    },
  ]
}

# Assign an RBAC role (system or custom) to the key from the role side.
resource "clickhouse_role_assignment" "monitoring" {
  role_id     = clickhouse_role.monitoring.id
  api_key_ids = [clickhouse_api_key.monitoring.id]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the API key.

### Optional

- `expire_at` (String) Timestamp (RFC3339) when the key expires. Omit for a key that never expires.
- `ip_access` (Attributes List) List of IP addresses/CIDRs allowed to use this key. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ip_access))
- `state` (String) State of the key: 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the API key (same value as key_id).
- `key_id` (String) Unique identifier for the API key.
- `key_secret` (String, Sensitive) The generated key secret. Returned only at creation and stored in state. Empty for imported keys.
- `key_suffix` (String) Last characters of the key, for identification.

<a id="nestedatt--ip_access"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ip_access`

Required:

- `source` (String) IP address or CIDR.

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional description of the entry.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# API keys can be imported by specifying the key ID.
# Note: key_secret is only returned at creation, so it will be empty after import.
terraform import clickhouse_api_key.example xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
```
